course.title: Build and Ship a Unicode-Safe Bookbot with Python 3.14
course.description: Python beginners with Git foundations who need to design, build, test, package, and defend a real command-line text-analysis product rather than copy a one-file tutorial. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate python 3.14.6 command lifecycle, git evidence, pathlike and file admission, byte and text i/o, explicit encoding and newline policy, unicode 17 normalization and segmentation, token definitions, frequency maps, deterministic ranking, code-point and grapheme distinctions, streaming and memory bounds, typed domain records, accessible human and machine reports, exceptions and exit statuses, argparse, unit and integration testing, profiling, packaging, entry points, security, release, rollback, and production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-python evidence using original fixed fixtures plus explicit local-filesystem, terminal, subprocess, git, packaging, installation, platform, load, security, accessibility, recovery, and release transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative authentic bookbot deliveries and a performance-based unfamiliar-corpus defense examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves Git state, input authority and identity, declared text policy, deterministic Python behavior, bounded resources, observable output, accessible diagnostics, changed-case tests, failure and recovery evidence, and explicit browser-versus-native transfer limits before adding one Bookbot boundary.

Browser Python uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure functions. It does not claim real filesystem, terminal, subprocess, Git, packaging, installation, platform, race, native profiler, load, or release behavior; those require controlled transfer evidence.

Passing work requires a stable scenario and artifact identity, prediction, intermediate data evidence, at least one changed Unicode or file case, at least one rejected case, exact stdout, stderr, and status expectations where applicable, tests that detect a deliberate defect, and a named owner for remaining risk.

competencies.statement: Define the report user, decision, input corpus, output contract, consequence of error, and observable success before coding.
misconceptions: Printing any word total is a complete Bookbot outcome.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define the report user, decision, input corpus, output contract, consequence of error, and observable success before coding.

competencies.statement: Record text owner, license or permission, source, version, checksum, language, and allowed use before analysis.
misconceptions: Text that can be downloaded is automatically authorized for redistribution.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record text owner, license or permission, source, version, checksum, language, and allowed use before analysis.

competencies.statement: Inspect Git status, branch, remotes, configuration origin, tracked files, ignore rules, and baseline tests before editing.
misconceptions: A clean-looking editor proves the repository has no uncommitted work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect Git status, branch, remotes, configuration origin, tracked files, ignore rules, and baseline tests before editing.

competencies.statement: Translate stakeholder needs into runnable behavior, changed cases, error cases, report examples, and acceptance gates.
misconceptions: A task is done when its happy-path output looks plausible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate stakeholder needs into runnable behavior, changed cases, error cases, report examples, and acceptance gates.

competencies.statement: Separate source, transformation, test, command, package, release, and stakeholder evidence so one green signal cannot hide another failure.
misconceptions: A passing unit test proves the installed command and report are correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate source, transformation, test, command, package, release, and stakeholder evidence so one green signal cannot hide another failure.

competencies.statement: Keep import-safe definitions separate from the __main__ execution boundary and return an exit status from main.
misconceptions: Code at module top level is harmless because it runs only from the command line.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep import-safe definitions separate from the __main__ execution boundary and return an exit status from main.

competencies.statement: Decompose admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, and orchestration into explicit contracts.
misconceptions: More tiny functions automatically create a better design.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Decompose admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, and orchestration into explicit contracts.

competencies.statement: Keep deterministic text transformations pure and pass filesystem, stream, clock, and terminal effects through a thin shell.
misconceptions: Pure functions cannot participate in a real command-line program.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep deterministic text transformations pure and pass filesystem, stream, clock, and terminal effects through a thin shell.

competencies.statement: Trace each value from argument through bytes, text, tokens, statistics, report records, and output without hidden mutation.
misconceptions: Python names own independent copies of every mutable value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace each value from argument through bytes, text, tokens, statistics, report records, and output without hidden mutation.

competencies.statement: Retain invocation, tested revision, input identity, stdout, stderr, exit status, duration, and environment boundary as one run record.
misconceptions: Captured stdout alone is enough to reproduce a command result.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retain invocation, tested revision, input identity, stdout, stderr, exit status, duration, and environment boundary as one run record.

competencies.statement: Accept PathLike input, preserve the user-facing path, and distinguish lexical path composition from filesystem resolution.
misconceptions: Path.resolve is merely prettier string normalization.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Accept PathLike input, preserve the user-facing path, and distinguish lexical path composition from filesystem resolution.

competencies.statement: Verify existence, regular-file type, readability, and race-aware open behavior before analysis.
misconceptions: Anything for which Path.exists returns true is a safe text file.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify existence, regular-file type, readability, and race-aware open behavior before analysis.

competencies.statement: Enforce an explicit allowed-root and symlink policy using resolved ancestry evidence rather than prefix strings.
misconceptions: A path beginning with the allowed directory string cannot escape it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce an explicit allowed-root and symlink policy using resolved ancestry evidence rather than prefix strings.

competencies.statement: Reject inputs beyond a declared byte budget before unbounded allocation and preserve actual versus allowed size evidence.
misconceptions: Character count is an adequate pre-read file-size limit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reject inputs beyond a declared byte budget before unbounded allocation and preserve actual versus allowed size evidence.

competencies.statement: Record stable path, size, modification observation, digest purpose, and open-file identity limits for reproducible analysis.
misconceptions: A filename uniquely identifies unchanged content over time.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record stable path, size, modification observation, digest purpose, and open-file identity limits for reproducible analysis.

competencies.statement: Distinguish raw bytes, decoded str values, code points, grapheme clusters, and serialized output at every boundary.
misconceptions: A Python string is the bytes stored in the source file.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish raw bytes, decoded str values, code points, grapheme clusters, and serialized output at every boundary.

competencies.statement: Choose and document UTF-8 or another declared encoding instead of relying on locale-dependent defaults.
misconceptions: Modern systems always use UTF-8 as the default text encoding.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose and document UTF-8 or another declared encoding instead of relying on locale-dependent defaults.

competencies.statement: Select strict rejection, replacement, ignore, or surrogate handling from the stakeholder evidence duty and expose data loss.
misconceptions: Replacing malformed bytes is always the most user-friendly choice.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select strict rejection, replacement, ignore, or surrogate handling from the stakeholder evidence duty and expose data loss.

competencies.statement: Test LF, CRLF, CR, final-newline, blank-line, and universal-newline behavior without changing semantic counts accidentally.
misconceptions: splitlines and split on newline always return the same records.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test LF, CRLF, CR, final-newline, blank-line, and universal-newline behavior without changing semantic counts accidentally.

competencies.statement: Use context management and narrow exception scopes so handles close and the causal error remains observable.
misconceptions: The operating system will promptly close every forgotten file handle.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use context management and narrow exception scopes so handles close and the causal error remains observable.

competencies.statement: Choose NFC, NFD, NFKC, NFKD, or no normalization from the comparison goal and version the decision.
misconceptions: NFKC preserves every distinction that readers and identifiers may need.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose NFC, NFD, NFKC, NFKD, or no normalization from the comparison goal and version the decision.

competencies.statement: Use casefold for declared caseless matching and test expansions and language-sensitive limitations rather than assuming lower is equivalent.
misconceptions: str.lower and str.casefold always produce the same comparison key.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use casefold for declared caseless matching and test expansions and language-sensitive limitations rather than assuming lower is equivalent.

competencies.statement: Retain original spans beside normalized comparison keys so reports remain inspectable and reversible.
misconceptions: Once counts are correct, original spelling and offsets no longer matter.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retain original spans beside normalized comparison keys so reports remain inspectable and reversible.

competencies.statement: Test precomposed and decomposed sequences, combining marks, emoji sequences, and zero-width characters as explicit cases.
misconceptions: One visible character always occupies one Python string element.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test precomposed and decomposed sequences, combining marks, emoji sequences, and zero-width characters as explicit cases.

competencies.statement: Record Unicode database and policy versions and rerun a changed fixture when either changes.
misconceptions: Unicode behavior is timeless and cannot alter a report between Python releases.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record Unicode database and policy versions and rerun a changed fixture when either changes.

competencies.statement: Define a word for the stakeholder, language set, punctuation, identifiers, numbers, contractions, and hyphenated forms before counting.
misconceptions: Every reader and programming library shares one universal definition of word.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define a word for the stakeholder, language set, punctuation, identifiers, numbers, contractions, and hyphenated forms before counting.

competencies.statement: Compare whitespace, regular-expression, Unicode-boundary, dictionary, and language-tailored segmentation against labeled cases.
misconceptions: One regular expression can correctly tokenize every writing system.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare whitespace, regular-expression, Unicode-boundary, dictionary, and language-tailored segmentation against labeled cases.

competencies.statement: Specify leading, trailing, internal, curly-apostrophe, dash, and repeated-punctuation behavior with changed examples.
misconceptions: Removing all punctuation cannot change word meaning or count.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Specify leading, trailing, internal, curly-apostrophe, dash, and repeated-punctuation behavior with changed examples.

competencies.statement: Return original start and end offsets with normalized token keys so count evidence can be inspected in source.
misconceptions: A normalized token value is enough to locate its occurrence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Return original start and end offsets with normalized token keys so count evidence can be inspected in source.

competencies.statement: Enforce nonempty tokens, monotonic nonoverlapping spans, declared normalization, and total-count reconciliation.
misconceptions: If no exception occurs, tokenizer output is structurally valid.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce nonempty tokens, monotonic nonoverlapping spans, declared normalization, and total-count reconciliation.

competencies.statement: Build frequencies with explicit dictionary or Counter updates and explain missing-key and zero-count semantics.
misconceptions: Counter and an ordinary dictionary behave identically for every operation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build frequencies with explicit dictionary or Counter updates and explain missing-key and zero-count semantics.

competencies.statement: Return a fresh result or an explicitly owned mutable result and prevent hidden cross-run state.
misconceptions: Clearing a local variable also clears every alias to the same dictionary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Return a fresh result or an explicitly owned mutable result and prevent hidden cross-run state.

competencies.statement: Assert that nonnegative frequencies sum to the accepted token count and preserve excluded-token evidence.
misconceptions: A plausible most-common list proves all tokens were counted.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assert that nonnegative frequencies sum to the accepted token count and preserve excluded-token evidence.

competencies.statement: Merge partial counters associatively and test partition independence for streaming and parallel transfer.
misconceptions: Adding independently normalized counters is safe even when their token policies differ.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Merge partial counters associatively and test partition independence for streaming and parallel transfer.

competencies.statement: Derive expected time and space cost from tokens and vocabulary before measuring implementation behavior.
misconceptions: Dictionary counting becomes quadratic as the number of tokens grows.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive expected time and space cost from tokens and vocabulary before measuring implementation behavior.

competencies.statement: Declare primary count direction, secondary token collation, normalization, locale boundary, and exact tie policy.
misconceptions: Descending counts automatically define a deterministic order for ties.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Declare primary count direction, secondary token collation, normalization, locale boundary, and exact tie policy.

competencies.statement: Express mixed ascending and descending order with an explicit key or stable multi-pass sort and trace tuple comparison.
misconceptions: reverse=True reverses only the first sort component.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Express mixed ascending and descending order with an explicit key or stable multi-pass sort and trace tuple comparison.

competencies.statement: Use and test Python stable sorting when earlier order is intentionally retained within equal keys.
misconceptions: Stable sorting means equal items may be returned in arbitrary order.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use and test Python stable sorting when earlier order is intentionally retained within equal keys.

competencies.statement: Validate k, rank the complete eligible set or use a justified selection algorithm, and specify ties at the cutoff.
misconceptions: Slicing the first k dictionary entries before sorting returns the top k.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate k, rank the complete eligible set or use a justified selection algorithm, and specify ties at the cutoff.

competencies.statement: Compare repeated and partitioned runs byte-for-byte and explain any locale, Unicode, or policy dependency.
misconceptions: Deterministic output matters only to automated tests, not users.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare repeated and partitioned runs byte-for-byte and explain any locale, Unicode, or policy dependency.

competencies.statement: Distinguish bytes, code points, extended grapheme clusters, display cells, letters, and stakeholder-defined characters.
misconceptions: len(text) always equals the number of characters a reader sees.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish bytes, code points, extended grapheme clusters, display cells, letters, and stakeholder-defined characters.

competencies.statement: Use Unicode categories or explicit predicates to separate letters, marks, numbers, punctuation, symbols, separators, and controls.
misconceptions: str.isalpha identifies every unit a reading report should call a character.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Unicode categories or explicit predicates to separate letters, marks, numbers, punctuation, symbols, separators, and controls.

competencies.statement: State whether character frequencies operate on original or normalized text, preserve case, and include or exclude marks.
misconceptions: Character frequency has one obvious definition independent of normalization.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State whether character frequencies operate on original or normalized text, preserve case, and include or exclude marks.

competencies.statement: Name the standard-library limitation for full grapheme segmentation and require a pinned evaluated implementation in native transfer.
misconceptions: Python slicing cannot divide a user-perceived character.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Name the standard-library limitation for full grapheme segmentation and require a pinned evaluated implementation in native transfer.

competencies.statement: Label every metric with unit, inclusion policy, input version, and reconciliation or non-reconciliation rule.
misconceptions: A report can call every text unit characters as long as the number is correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Label every metric with unit, inclusion policy, input version, and reconciliation or non-reconciliation rule.

competencies.statement: Process lines or bounded chunks incrementally and keep only state required by the declared report.
misconceptions: Using a for loop over a file guarantees the whole pipeline has bounded memory.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Process lines or bounded chunks incrementally and keep only state required by the declared report.

competencies.statement: Carry a bounded token fragment across chunk boundaries and prove concatenated-chunk equivalence.
misconceptions: Each chunk can be tokenized independently without affecting results.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Carry a bounded token fragment across chunk boundaries and prove concatenated-chunk equivalence.

competencies.statement: Use an incremental decoder or text stream that preserves multibyte sequences and explicit error policy.
misconceptions: UTF-8 bytes may be split at any position and decoded separately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use an incremental decoder or text stream that preserves multibyte sequences and explicit error policy.

competencies.statement: Set byte, vocabulary, token-fragment, line, output, and elapsed-time budgets with rejection behavior.
misconceptions: Streaming eliminates every possible memory-growth path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set byte, vocabulary, token-fragment, line, output, and elapsed-time budgets with rejection behavior.

competencies.statement: Compare batch and streaming results across adversarial split points, malformed data, and final fragments.
misconceptions: One ordinary file is sufficient evidence that streaming matches batch analysis.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare batch and streaming results across adversarial split points, malformed data, and final fragments.

competencies.statement: Model input identity, policy, token, statistics, ranking row, and report metadata with explicit dataclasses or typed records.
misconceptions: A dictionary is self-documenting because its keys are visible at runtime.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model input identity, policy, token, statistics, ranking row, and report metadata with explicit dataclasses or typed records.

competencies.statement: Use immutable records where post-computation mutation would invalidate retained evidence.
misconceptions: frozen dataclasses make every referenced nested object immutable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use immutable records where post-computation mutation would invalidate retained evidence.

competencies.statement: Give each pipeline stage typed input, output, invariant, failure, and version contracts.
misconceptions: A pipeline is correct when data reaches the final print call.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Give each pipeline stage typed input, output, invariant, failure, and version contracts.

competencies.statement: Inject readers, policies, formatters, and streams at narrow boundaries without replacing real integration evidence with mocks.
misconceptions: Dependency injection means every object must be hidden behind a class interface.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inject readers, policies, formatters, and streams at narrow boundaries without replacing real integration evidence with mocks.

competencies.statement: Version JSON or CSV report schemas, preserve types and ordering, and reject non-finite or ambiguous values.
misconceptions: Python repr output is a stable cross-language data format.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Version JSON or CSV report schemas, preserve types and ordering, and reject non-finite or ambiguous values.

competencies.statement: Order title, input identity, policy, totals, rankings, limitations, and next actions around the reader decision.
misconceptions: A report is good when it contains every available metric.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Order title, input identity, policy, totals, rankings, limitations, and next actions around the reader decision.

competencies.statement: Reserve stdout for requested report data and stderr for diagnostics while keeping exit status consistent.
misconceptions: Users cannot observe whether output was written to stdout or stderr.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reserve stdout for requested report data and stderr for diagnostics while keeping exit status consistent.

competencies.statement: Use a declared width or detected terminal width with minimums, wrapping, long-token policy, and snapshot tests.
misconceptions: Hard-coded 80-column formatting is accessible on every terminal.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use a declared width or detected terminal width with minimums, wrapping, long-token policy, and snapshot tests.

competencies.statement: Keep every status and distinction understandable without color and honor plain or no-color operation.
misconceptions: ANSI color improves accessibility even when it is the only error signal.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep every status and distinction understandable without color and honor plain or no-color operation.

competencies.statement: Provide labeled rows, deterministic plain text, and machine-readable output as equivalent evidence rather than screenshots.
misconceptions: A visually aligned table is automatically understandable to assistive technology.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide labeled rows, deterministic plain text, and machine-readable output as equivalent evidence rather than screenshots.

competencies.statement: Translate expected path, admission, decoding, policy, and output failures into narrow domain errors without erasing causes.
misconceptions: Every built-in exception should be exposed directly to command users.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate expected path, admission, decoding, policy, and output failures into narrow domain errors without erasing causes.

competencies.statement: Write concise actionable diagnostics containing operation and safe context while retaining a debuggable causal chain.
misconceptions: A full traceback is always the clearest message for end users.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write concise actionable diagnostics containing operation and safe context while retaining a debuggable causal chain.

competencies.statement: Map success, usage error, input error, analysis error, and output error to documented stable statuses.
misconceptions: Any nonzero exit code communicates the same recovery action.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map success, usage error, input error, analysis error, and output error to documented stable statuses.

competencies.statement: Render before replace or write through an atomic strategy when a report file must not become partially valid.
misconceptions: Closing a partially written report makes it safe to consume.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Render before replace or write through an atomic strategy when a report file must not become partially valid.

competencies.statement: Give a bounded next action, preserve original inputs, and distinguish retryable from policy or data failures.
misconceptions: Retrying every file failure is a safe recovery plan.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Give a bounded next action, preserve original inputs, and distinguish retryable from policy or data failures.

competencies.statement: Define positional input and explicit encoding, token, ranking, format, width, and limit options from user tasks.
misconceptions: Every implementation setting belongs in the public CLI.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define positional input and explicit encoding, token, ranking, format, width, and limit options from user tasks.

competencies.statement: Reject invalid ranges, conflicting flags, unsupported choices, and missing values at the parser boundary with usage evidence.
misconceptions: Type conversion alone validates a command-line argument semantically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reject invalid ranges, conflicting flags, unsupported choices, and missing values at the parser boundary with usage evidence.

competencies.statement: Write task-oriented usage, descriptions, metavariables, defaults, units, examples, exit behavior, and accessibility notes.
misconceptions: Auto-generated option names are sufficient command documentation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write task-oriented usage, descriptions, metavariables, defaults, units, examples, exit behavior, and accessibility notes.

competencies.statement: Parse supplied argument lists separately from sys.argv and return a configuration record for testing.
misconceptions: Testing a parser requires launching a subprocess for every option.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse supplied argument lists separately from sys.argv and return a configuration record for testing.

competencies.statement: Control Python 3.14 suggestion, color, abbreviation, and execution-name behavior explicitly where reproducibility requires it.
misconceptions: Argparse output and defaults never change between Python versions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Control Python 3.14 suggestion, color, abbreviation, and execution-name behavior explicitly where reproducibility requires it.

competencies.statement: Partition tests across pure units, file integration, CLI behavior, report snapshots, package installation, and stakeholder acceptance.
misconceptions: Unit tests alone prove the complete command works.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Partition tests across pure units, file integration, CLI behavior, report snapshots, package installation, and stakeholder acceptance.

competencies.statement: Use named table and subtest cases for empty, punctuation, Unicode, malformed, large, tied, and boundary inputs.
misconceptions: One representative fixture can cover every important text behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use named table and subtest cases for empty, punctuation, Unicode, malformed, large, tied, and boundary inputs.

competencies.statement: Use real temporary files and directories for filesystem contracts and reserve mocks for narrow failure injection.
misconceptions: Mocking open is more realistic than reading a temporary file.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use real temporary files and directories for filesystem contracts and reserve mocks for narrow failure injection.

competencies.statement: Test stdout, stderr, status, help, invalid arguments, working directory, and installed entry point as observable CLI behavior.
misconceptions: Calling main directly proves shell invocation behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test stdout, stderr, status, help, invalid arguments, working directory, and installed entry point as observable CLI behavior.

competencies.statement: Prove tests fail under a deliberate changed behavior and protect against order, locale, encoding, platform, and stale-fixture coupling.
misconceptions: Code coverage percentage establishes that assertions detect defects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove tests fail under a deliberate changed behavior and protect against order, locale, encoding, platform, and stale-fixture coupling.

competencies.statement: State workload, input scale, expected complexity, environment, metric, budget, and correctness invariant before measuring.
misconceptions: Any microbenchmark result is meaningful without a workload model.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State workload, input scale, expected complexity, environment, metric, budget, and correctness invariant before measuring.

competencies.statement: Use repeated controlled timing, warmup decisions, robust summaries, and noise disclosure rather than one stopwatch value.
misconceptions: The smallest single timing is the most accurate performance estimate.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use repeated controlled timing, warmup decisions, robust summaries, and noise disclosure rather than one stopwatch value.

competencies.statement: Use cProfile or a justified profiler to locate cumulative work before selecting a repair.
misconceptions: The slowest-looking source line is necessarily the dominant cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use cProfile or a justified profiler to locate cumulative work before selecting a repair.

competencies.statement: Measure peak allocations or process memory for batch and stream paths against vocabulary and output growth.
misconceptions: Linear-time code automatically uses bounded memory.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure peak allocations or process memory for batch and stream paths against vocabulary and output growth.

competencies.statement: Gate representative input slices on correctness first and practical time and memory thresholds second.
misconceptions: A faster result may be accepted even if Unicode or ranking output changes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Gate representative input slices on correctness first and practical time and memory thresholds second.

competencies.statement: Separate import package, tests, fixtures, documentation, and build artifacts with a src layout and explicit package discovery.
misconceptions: A src layout is decorative and cannot expose import mistakes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate import package, tests, fixtures, documentation, and build artifacts with a src layout and explicit package discovery.

competencies.statement: Declare canonical name, version policy, Python requirement, description, license, authorship, classifiers, URLs, and build system in pyproject.toml.
misconceptions: The build backend may safely guess all missing project metadata.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Declare canonical name, version policy, Python requirement, description, license, authorship, classifiers, URLs, and build system in pyproject.toml.

competencies.statement: Support python -m execution and an installed console script that call the same testable main boundary.
misconceptions: __main__.py and console_scripts must contain separate command implementations.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Support python -m execution and an installed console script that call the same testable main boundary.

competencies.statement: Build wheel and source distribution from an identified clean revision and inspect contents, metadata, and excluded fixtures.
misconceptions: A successful build command proves the artifact contains the intended files.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build wheel and source distribution from an identified clean revision and inspect contents, metadata, and excluded fixtures.

competencies.statement: Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output.
misconceptions: Tests run from the source checkout prove the wheel is installable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output.

competencies.statement: Threat-model paths, devices, links, huge lines, malformed encodings, control characters, output injection, resource exhaustion, and race limits.
misconceptions: A local command does not need an untrusted-input model.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Threat-model paths, devices, links, huge lines, malformed encodings, control characters, output injection, resource exhaustion, and race limits.

competencies.statement: Keep reports and logs to authorized metrics and safe identifiers, with explicit excerpt, retention, deletion, and sharing policy.
misconceptions: Text analytics output cannot expose sensitive source information.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep reports and logs to authorized metrics and safe identifiers, with explicit excerpt, retention, deletion, and sharing policy.

competencies.statement: Use a modern digest for content identity while distinguishing accidental-corruption evidence from authenticated provenance.
misconceptions: A matching SHA-256 digest proves who supplied a text.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use a modern digest for content identity while distinguishing accidental-corruption evidence from authenticated provenance.

competencies.statement: Gate immutable versioned artifacts on tests, accessibility, security, performance, clean installation, provenance, staged rollout, abort, and rollback evidence.
misconceptions: Reinstalling source from the current branch is a reliable rollback.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Gate immutable versioned artifacts on tests, accessibility, security, performance, clean installation, provenance, staged rollout, abort, and rollback evidence.

competencies.statement: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.
misconceptions: Passing the final happy-path demo makes Bookbot production ready.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.

credential.title: Build and Ship a Unicode-Safe Bookbot with Python 3.14 Certificate

modules.title: Bookbot Outcomes, Repository Baseline, and Evidence
modules.description: A volunteer digital library asks for a word-count script but has not defined the report user, authorized input, counting policy, failure behavior, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Learners cumulatively produce Bookbot outcome, corpus, repository, and evidence charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define the report user, decision, input corpus, output contract, consequence of error, and observable success before coding.
Record text owner, license or permission, source, version, checksum, language, and allowed use before analysis.
Inspect Git status, branch, remotes, configuration origin, tracked files, ignore rules, and baseline tests before editing.

modules.title: Command Lifecycle, Main Boundary, and Decomposition
modules.description: A one-file script reads global arguments during import, mixes file access with counting and printing, cannot be tested without exiting, and changes behavior when imported. Learners cumulatively produce testable command lifecycle and function map through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep import-safe definitions separate from the __main__ execution boundary and return an exit status from main.
Decompose admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, and orchestration into explicit contracts.
Keep deterministic text transformations pure and pass filesystem, stream, clock, and terminal effects through a thin shell.

modules.title: Paths, File Admission, Root Boundaries, and Size Budgets
modules.description: The command concatenates strings into paths, follows any symlink, accepts directories and devices, reads before checking size, and reports every failure as file not found. Learners cumulatively produce bounded file-admission policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Accept PathLike input, preserve the user-facing path, and distinguish lexical path composition from filesystem resolution.
Verify existence, regular-file type, readability, and race-aware open behavior before analysis.
Enforce an explicit allowed-root and symlink policy using resolved ancestry evidence rather than prefix strings.

modules.title: Bytes, Text I/O, Encodings, Newlines, and Cleanup
modules.description: A Bookbot relies on the platform default encoding, silently replaces invalid bytes, translates newlines differently by platform, reads the entire file twice, and leaks a handle on failure. Learners cumulatively produce explicit text-I/O boundary through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish raw bytes, decoded str values, code points, grapheme clusters, and serialized output at every boundary.
Choose and document UTF-8 or another declared encoding instead of relying on locale-dependent defaults.
Select strict rejection, replacement, ignore, or surrogate handling from the stakeholder evidence duty and expose data loss.

modules.title: Unicode Normalization, Case Folding, and Original Evidence
modules.description: Visually identical words count separately, compatibility characters collapse without disclosure, lowercasing misses caseless matches, and the report cannot link normalized tokens back to original text. Learners cumulatively produce versioned reversible Unicode policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose NFC, NFD, NFKC, NFKD, or no normalization from the comparison goal and version the decision.
Use casefold for declared caseless matching and test expansions and language-sensitive limitations rather than assuming lower is equivalent.
Retain original spans beside normalized comparison keys so reports remain inspectable and reversible.

modules.title: Word Definitions, Tokenization, Boundaries, and Offsets
modules.description: Whitespace splitting treats punctuation as words, a regex drops apostrophes and non-Latin scripts, hyphen policy changes totals silently, and empty tokens enter the frequency table. Learners cumulatively produce tested word and token contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define a word for the stakeholder, language set, punctuation, identifiers, numbers, contractions, and hyphenated forms before counting.
Compare whitespace, regular-expression, Unicode-boundary, dictionary, and language-tailored segmentation against labeled cases.
Specify leading, trailing, internal, curly-apostrophe, dash, and repeated-punctuation behavior with changed examples.

modules.title: Frequency Maps, Counter Semantics, and Reconciliation
modules.description: A counting loop resets existing entries, aliases a shared dictionary, includes empty tokens, sorts while mutating, and never reconciles token totals with frequency totals. Learners cumulatively produce inspectable frequency engine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Build frequencies with explicit dictionary or Counter updates and explain missing-key and zero-count semantics.
Return a fresh result or an explicitly owned mutable result and prevent hidden cross-run state.
Assert that nonnegative frequencies sum to the accepted token count and preserve excluded-token evidence.

modules.title: Ranking Keys, Stable Sorts, Ties, and Top-K Reports
modules.description: Equal-frequency words appear in hash-dependent order, reverse sorting reverses both count and token unexpectedly, top-k truncation happens before ranking, and repeated runs produce noisy diffs. Learners cumulatively produce deterministic ranked-frequency report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Declare primary count direction, secondary token collation, normalization, locale boundary, and exact tie policy.
Express mixed ascending and descending order with an explicit key or stable multi-pass sort and trace tuple comparison.
Use and test Python stable sorting when earlier order is intentionally retained within equal keys.

modules.title: Code Points, Grapheme Clusters, Character Categories, and Labels
modules.description: A report labels len(text) as characters, counts newline and combining marks as letters, merges case categories without policy, and presents incomparable metrics under one total. Learners cumulatively produce honestly labeled text-metrics panel through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish bytes, code points, extended grapheme clusters, display cells, letters, and stakeholder-defined characters.
Use Unicode categories or explicit predicates to separate letters, marks, numbers, punctuation, symbols, separators, and controls.
State whether character frequencies operate on original or normalized text, preserve case, and include or exclude marks.

modules.title: Streaming Analysis, Chunk Boundaries, and Resource Budgets
modules.description: A large-file mode still calls read_text, splits tokens at chunk edges, decodes partial UTF-8 incorrectly, retains every token, and claims bounded memory without measurement. Learners cumulatively produce bounded streaming analyzer through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Process lines or bounded chunks incrementally and keep only state required by the declared report.
Carry a bounded token fragment across chunk boundaries and prove concatenated-chunk equivalence.
Use an incremental decoder or text stream that preserves multibyte sequences and explicit error policy.

modules.title: Domain Records, Pure Pipeline Stages, and Serialization
modules.description: The program passes nested dictionaries with undocumented keys, mutates statistics during formatting, serializes Python repr output, and cannot isolate which stage changed a result. Learners cumulatively produce typed cumulative analysis pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model input identity, policy, token, statistics, ranking row, and report metadata with explicit dataclasses or typed records.
Use immutable records where post-computation mutation would invalidate retained evidence.
Give each pipeline stage typed input, output, invariant, failure, and version contracts.

modules.title: Human Reports, Machine Output, Terminal Width, and Accessibility
modules.description: The report encodes meaning only with color, clips long words, changes columns by terminal width, mixes diagnostics with JSON stdout, and gives screen-reader users an unlabeled number grid. Learners cumulatively produce accessible human and machine report system through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Order title, input identity, policy, totals, rankings, limitations, and next actions around the reader decision.
Reserve stdout for requested report data and stderr for diagnostics while keeping exit status consistent.
Use a declared width or detected terminal width with minimums, wrapping, long-token policy, and snapshot tests.

modules.title: Exceptions, Diagnostics, Exit Status, and Recovery
modules.description: The command catches Exception around the whole program, prints a traceback to stdout, exits zero after failure, hides the path and cause, and leaves a partial report behind. Learners cumulatively produce causal error and recovery contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Translate expected path, admission, decoding, policy, and output failures into narrow domain errors without erasing causes.
Write concise actionable diagnostics containing operation and safe context while retaining a debuggable causal chain.
Map success, usage error, input error, analysis error, and output error to documented stable statuses.

modules.title: Argparse Interface, Help, Options, and Usage Errors
modules.description: The parser abbreviates ambiguous flags, hides defaults and units, accepts negative limits, prints colored help into snapshots, and couples parse_args directly to analysis. Learners cumulatively produce testable user-facing command interface through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define positional input and explicit encoding, token, ranking, format, width, and limit options from user tasks.
Reject invalid ranges, conflicting flags, unsupported choices, and missing values at the parser boundary with usage evidence.
Write task-oriented usage, descriptions, metavariables, defaults, units, examples, exit behavior, and accessibility notes.

modules.title: Unit, Integration, CLI, Fixture, and Changed-Case Testing
modules.description: Tests assert only one public-domain English sample total, mock every filesystem call, compare unordered dictionaries, share mutable fixtures, and never run the installed command. Learners cumulatively produce risk-layered Bookbot test suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Partition tests across pure units, file integration, CLI behavior, report snapshots, package installation, and stakeholder acceptance.
Use named table and subtest cases for empty, punctuation, Unicode, malformed, large, tied, and boundary inputs.
Use real temporary files and directories for filesystem contracts and reserve mocks for narrow failure injection.

modules.title: Complexity, Timing, Profiling, Memory, and Capacity
modules.description: A team optimizes string concatenation from one warm run, reports elapsed time without input size, never measures memory, and ships a faster tokenizer that changes word policy. Learners cumulatively produce correctness-preserving capacity study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: State workload, input scale, expected complexity, environment, metric, budget, and correctness invariant before measuring.
Use repeated controlled timing, warmup decisions, robust summaries, and noise disclosure rather than one stopwatch value.
Use cProfile or a justified profiler to locate cumulative work before selecting a repair.

modules.title: Package Layout, Pyproject Metadata, Entry Points, and Reproducible Builds
modules.description: Bookbot works only from the repository root, imports an uninstalled local file, lacks metadata and version identity, builds from a dirty tree, and publishes a wheel never tested after installation. Learners cumulatively produce installable reproducible Bookbot package through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate import package, tests, fixtures, documentation, and build artifacts with a src layout and explicit package discovery.
Declare canonical name, version policy, Python requirement, description, license, authorship, classifiers, URLs, and build system in pyproject.toml.
Support python -m execution and an installed console script that call the same testable main boundary.

modules.title: Untrusted Inputs, Integrity, Privacy, Release, Rollback, and Defense
modules.description: A release analyzes arbitrary paths without limits, logs text excerpts, follows swapped symlinks, treats a digest as authenticity, cannot reproduce its wheel, and has no rollback or incident drill. Learners cumulatively produce production Bookbot release and residual-risk defense through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Threat-model paths, devices, links, huge lines, malformed encodings, control characters, output injection, resource exhaustion, and race limits.
Keep reports and logs to authorized metrics and safe identifiers, with explicit excerpt, retention, deletion, and sharing policy.
Use a modern digest for content identity while distinguishing accidental-corruption evidence from authenticated provenance.
